---
source: Demo Coach (fixture)
title: Sample volume landmarks talk
date: 2026-01-01
---

Most natural lifters make good progress with roughly ten to twenty hard sets
per muscle group each week, spread across at least two sessions. Going past
your recoverable volume for too long shows up as stalled lifts and poor sleep.

When recovery markers are down for several days, cut set volume before you cut
load on the bar. Planned deloads usually mean cutting weekly volume by about
forty to fifty percent while keeping some heavier work so technique stays sharp.

Easy cardio days should stay easy. Jumping weekly running mileage too fast is
a common way to get hurt — treat large week-over-week jumps as a red flag.
